channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The US Treasury is naked shorting oil futures — 51 replies — 204.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US Treasury is naked shorting oil futures

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-03-17 02:33 UTC

OP

I’ve been watching this shit all day and it’s wild af. Oil futures get hyper dumped in giant chunks, which causes the price of spot to react, like the retarded dog on a leash it is. The thing is - everything is getting worse; tankers aren’t sailing, oil reserves are getting more dire, and there is no resolution in sight.

Meanwhile someone is hyper dumping oil futures like their job depends on it. Because it does. The fucking US Treasury is desperately trying to keep oil prices in check by gaming the market, but all they’re really doing is destroying the legitimacy of the futures market entirely. This little game works a few times until the larger market realizes what’s going on and literally just abandons the futures market for spot product.

Unfucking believable how corrupt and stupid this administration is.

Early replies (first 3)

61992408
Maybe the retardmaxers were right about needing to shut off our brains to make money

61992411
Mossad is also trying to FUD oil all over social media.

61992416
>>61992372
Next time, don't get your investing thesises from seething third worlders and jam boys.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993089
>>61992372
Midterms are all that matter, nigger. Life is about daytrading someone else's future

61993104
>>61992372
Also, if this is what it takes to break all foreign trust and dependance on the usa, so be it.

61993188
>>61992372
This is 100% happening. Probably using HFT cutouts like Jane Street. "Capitalism" is fake and gay. We need real capitalism.

61993233
>>61992372
Now you understand what has been happening in gold and silver markets for decades.

61993270
>>61993233
If a market can be successfully 'manipulated' for decades, doesn't the 'manipulation' simply become the actual market? What's the functional difference? Real manipulation should be impossible over longer periods of time due to market self-correction mechanisms.

61993299
>>61993270
Naked shorting is only a problem if someone tries to take delivery of the underlying physical good. For a long time buyers were happy settling for cash and not taking delivery. Recent price moves in precious metals are because of demand for physical. That is the self-correcting feature and its playing out slowly.

61993347
>>61992372
They have no choice but to do this. They need the petrodollar to survive, which requires the military operations in Iran to succeed. If the US government cannot achieve Iranian regime change before it becomes politically/economically impossible to continue the war, then we will be forever changed as a nation. Retreating from Iran almost certainly means the collapse of US dominance in the region, and thus the collapse of the petrodollar.
Yes the admin is corrupt and stupid, but they also have no choice. This is really the calm before the storm my friend, if the petrodollar collapses then dollar dominance will follow, and then the US will actually need to answer for its national debt. We're in for some rough times ahead.

61993408
>>61992717
>suddenly cutting off 20% of the world’s oil supply
Literally wrong. There's ~2 trillion barrels of oil in the supply. 20M barrels is not 20%. It's 20% of the oil produced per day. Which is a literal fucking nothing in the supply.
It's 20% of 5% of the supply
Which is 1% of the supply.
Literal.
Fucking.
Nothing.
But that oil is still being produced, there's still ~95m barrels being produced daily. And the countries that would be buying that oil still have plenty of reserves.
>Can I get a job fed posting?
No, sand people are barred.
>>61992995
The Iranians didn't destroy 20million barrels worth of production. The production remains largely unchanged.
What was affected, however, was the ability to get that production onto the market through the strait of homos.

An extremely small percentage of oil production was lost. Maybe like 3%.
>Oh and on top of that gulf countries have nowhere to store the oil and are reducing their production as well.
Lmao don't you see how this contradicts your point?
>We have so much oil we can't store it!
>BUT THERE'S A HUGE SUPPLY RISK!!!

There is no supply shock.

61993426
>>61992372
They’ve been doing the same thing to silver for the last 20 years, and getting caught doing so and fined btw

61993437
>>61993408
oh ok thats why a ton of countries will be running out in 10 days

61993444
>>61992607
you're a fucking retard lmao

61993497
>>61993437
You're full of shit. Most countries still have around a year of reserves. And their imports of oil have not been cut to zero.
It's just that oil leaving the middle east is being held hostage by the literal terrorists.

But there's still plenty of oil around the world that can be exported to those countries. Not only that, the strait is simply the cheapest way to get the oil out, there are other options. Just with less throughput.
>>61993444
Prove me wrong.
Oh wait, you literally cannot.

61993514
>>61992372
>naked shorting oil
While you are right you are also wrong, it is not naked, they just steal the oil if they are getting called.

61993520
>>61992372
people like you spreading this idea are so beyond retarded. the US is the largest oil producers in the world now and the SPR is releasing 172M barrels of oil. the US Treasury could short futures and fucking deliver the oil to the market this way. its not a naked short you fucking moron THEY HAVE THE OIL TO DELIVER.

61993675
>>61993408
they can't store it because they have no storage facilities. Where they are supposed to put all that oil that they can't put on tankers?

61993780
We've been in an economic recession for months now. Like covid and 2008 levels. Oil is about to plummet sub $50

61993797
Dont look up onion futures

61993807
>>61992416
Such a forced glownigger meme
Why can't glowniggers say shitskin even on pol? Because they are shitskins?

61993827
Buy 10x leveraged calls faggot

61993836
>>61993675
Anon... It's the middle east that has limited storage because they cannot export. Their storage is getting filled to capacity and they're having a USOIL 2020 mini-repeat.

Just think about what you're saying for like two seconds.

61993838
>>61992372
>The US Treasury is naked shorting oil futures

Futures contracts are derivatives. You can't naked short a derivative. What the Treasury Department is doing is selling huge amounts of unhedged crude oil futures contracts to open.

Precious metals have faced an "Inverse Plunge Protection Team" at Treasury since perhaps the late 90's that has criminally suppressed prices. Ironically under Scott Bessent, at least up to now Trump 47 has been spectacularly good for precious metals.

61993859
>>61993299
To be fair if Scotty is naked shorting he can just fulfill by purchasing at market rates. Futures would still be fulfilled, but the paper rate would stay low. That’s my taxes at work. It could work in theory, the gov would just be blowing all of our tax money to manipulate the market.

61993872
>>61992607
Glownigger

61993974
>>61993807

61994159
>>61993408
Hey may not be a glowie, he may just be genuinely retarded

61994194
>>61992372
welcome to the (((naked short wealth extraction machine)))! it's highly illegal and destructive to any nation but here we are again: this is the official m.o. of our (((financial leadership))).
1) hype thing up with media apparatus
2) let it run up and hyp it some more
3) conspire with your (((fellow bankers))) against the people and premeditate market manipulation and massive financial fraud
4) together naked short the shit out the hyped thing and extract as much wealth as possible out of the commoners
5) call your buddies at the (((SEC))) to confess and pay your microbially tiny slap on the wrist punishment fee
6) then continue to suppress the stock forever without ever having to close your naked shorts thus trapping commoners forever
7) call (((the president))) to brag about making the green line go up for rich people to get and award
8) repeat

61994198
>>61994159
Name one thing I'm wrong about.
Pro tip:
>You literally cannot.

61994204
>>61992372
>legitimacy of the futures market entirely
AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHA, this retard thinks futures are real

61994216
>>61992607
Only a glownigger or a tranny would say delulu and honestly the trannys don't use it much

61994228
>>61994216
>What is postirony

item.title: Chilling w/ Rich People — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chilling w/ Rich People

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-03-17 02:27 UTC

OP

I just spent 3 days working/chilling with people worth over 100 mil in a major city. I am a poor.

AMA

Early replies (first 3)

61984154
>>61984144
Rich people are always fascinated by my poorfag stories.

61984159
Zased. One time I spent a few days chilling with my rich Jordanian friends in Cinncinati and they gave me a 2025 m3 comp to drive while I was there and took me to an expensive steakhouse

Unfortunately my friend’s whore wife’s cousin got caught funneling money from his business and the whore sided with the cousin and ended up divorcing my friend, haven’t really see him since then

61984200
>>61984154
These ones didn't care about mine. They just say "oh cool" and move on, that was last time I chilled with them. We bonded with one because he gets employees from Eastern Europe and underpays them and I am from Eastern Europe, so I made jokes about how he has slave labor, he enjoyed that one. I grew up in a room in a communal Soviet block with a shared bathroom with no shower, so I have good ones, like the neighbor prostitutes autistic son drawing on the walls with shit while she is whoring in the room next door. Her titties were fine though, sometimes I wonder if she would have let me touch them at 7 years old, because why not, whore anyway.

>>61984159
I am not that poor biw, I am just old and a wagie, my poor friends have new Supras and 700whp MR2s with smooth boost that keep up with ZR1s, so M3 comp wouldn't be an event for me.

The rich fags absolutely do not flex cars since it's considered tacky. A previous story from chilling with them is that one of the kids at the company cashed in his equity and got a 718 and then the founder brutally smoked him in his Plaid. Note - I am not a fan of Tesla, but it was still funny. I think the kid didn't really know how to drop gears to drag either, so it was just bad.

But yea, these fags flex their private jet (emphasis on jet, not plane), but they still share it.

Oh, and they made fun of Cincinnati. They went to Jeff Ruby's and one of them cut up the table cloth and ran around with it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61984482
>>61984144
I get the impression people like that are rather insulated and uninteresting in anything not related to their exact field of work. As in having the most normie (or at best pseud) views on politics, philosophy, history, etc. I assume thats why people like crowly and rasputin where able to walk circles around the upperclass in spite being insane
Does that line up with your experience?

61984538
>>61984482
It's a little bit more nuanced than that.

>I get the impression people like that are rather insulated and uninteresting in anything not related to their exact field of work
This is largely correct. They are extremely focused on their work and spend most of their day working on their business. Note that "business" isn't some vague word that MBA kids think it is. If they work on servers, they spend all day working on servers. If they work on small satellites, they spend all day on it. That does includes sales, conferences, etc.

The one difference I found between them and me (and other loser nerds) is that it's not conceptual either - they don't spend time chasing vague theory, like "but what if blah", they always ask what's in the market no, what's realistic, and is it actually going to give an advantage over competitors and/or for sales.

They are also obsessed over money and flexing on their other rich friends, but again, literally just with the number, not any material things.

>As in having the most normie (or at best pseud) views on politics, philosophy, history, etc.
They don't really do the theorizing part that we do on here, but that's not to say they can't think critically / consider the other possibilities. Let's say you say that 9/11 was a false flag. They will just "sure, that makes sense, governments have done insider t attacks to drum up support for military actions". If you go more into the /pol/ side of things about the owner, how he wasn't there, what country he is associated with, etc, they will also not dismiss it, just say it makes sense. But they are not going to dwell on it or speak about it for days.

They also have conversations like "The US is more communist than X European country, because in X country we couldn't get funding to do research, but the US gave us a bil so they don't fall behind globally."
(cont.)

61984541
tldr: Not dumb, but definitely not dwelling on shit like "are aliens real" - they understand that it doesn't matter to them day-to-day.

>>61984482
> I assume thats why people like crowly and rasputin where able to walk circles around the upperclass in spite being insane
Now this is the key point which is wrong - both Crowley and Rasputin ran circles around idiots. There is a clear distinction between idiotic old money (and not all of them are) and people who do shit and can differentiate bs from fiction.

I am going to pretend I am rich and go high level - Rasputin ran circles around spoiled court-ladies and the empress who didn't do shit, were bored, open to mysticism and didn't get good dick. They WANTED to believe him and had nothing on the line (well, except the whole imperial family eventually losing influence and being assassinated), but you get the gist.

Crowley never achieved shit after he became an occultist - he rode on his old money and reputation as a chess player and alpinist, but these guys would absolutely NOT believe him, because the immediate question is "what practical problems do you solve?" and the answer is "nothing", and that ends the convo.

P.S. Comte of St.Germain would probably be a better example, but they wouldn't believe him either.

Short answer - no, and not just for occultists, any other bsers.

Also, people always say "network with rich people" - the reality is that only works if you have real alignment, like they sell serves and you sell RAM, then they MIGHT go with you over a competitor if you can talk through a decent deal and they know they can knock on your door to solve problems. But there is never any magic "networking" effect.

Hard no to your original question, unless they are the true generate multi-generation wealth which is actually rare, because they still have to appear competitive to peers.

61984557
>>61984482
And finally, there is a good chance they don't really know who Crowley and St.Germain are and haven't heard of them, because they didn't spend time reading occult shit to begin with.

They will know of Rasputin exactly as a history book describes them, but you and I spend way too much time reading useless shit. In that way, we "fell for it" more than they did, since we are researching something that essentially has no value just so we can mumble obscure shit. I will say that because of looking into, we are probably more resilient to cults, silly ideas, etc. However, the rich fags I am talking about just dismiss them outright. They are literally working on their business or going out with models (or their wives in many cases) while you and I shitpost here. And it adds up. I work really hard, but I still shitpost in between, they don't. They really do "work hard/party hard". I thought I worked hard too, but the fact that my top entertainment is posting here does kill motivation and I drag work out because I haven't hard real success. They stay motivated.

61984690
>>61984557
> I work really hard, but I still shitpost in between, they don't.

they've got that grindset mindset which is like the left end of the IQ bell curve really, isn't it? they end up being about as interesting to talk to as a delivery driver, at the end of the day.

And they never ever stop watching every word that comes out of their mouths. There's never a slip of the tongue. Just like low IQ retards are sweating over 'err did i just say the wrong thing?? no u said the wrong thing! no wait did i say it', so are rich people.

61985012
>>61984538
>>61984541
>>61984557
Interesting, thanks for the insights anon!

61987443
>>61984538
Seems like an awful way to live man.

61987540
>>61987443
Well of course, they're still dumb normalfags even if they're rich. None of that shit applies to rich autists who have made it through advanced nomismancy.

61987543
Thanks for the info OP. Always nice to learn more about those kinds of people.
Were there a lot of jews?

61987556
>>61984144
Did the richfags seem happy? I have this friend, he's among the top 10 richfags (old, old money) in our Town of 50.000, and he's always melancholic, overworked, lonely and it gets worse when he's drunk. You'd think Richfags have to be happier on average

61989087
>>61984144
they like having you around in the same way a pretty girl likes having an ugly friend.

61989355
>>61984538
Are they vaxxed?

61989483
>>61984144
How does it feel to be a gay prostitute and think the depraved self-extinction you experience is luxury?

61989494
>>61984144
Cool, who cares?
Like, are they giving you money to stop working?

That'd be the only reason I'd hang with them.

61989536
There are all kinds of different rich people. Conspiratard fellow travelers sometimes do get rich and they don't stop being conspiratards. If they're the business type they probably turn it off for longer periods, but my conspiratarded rich friends are VERY likely to sit up all night chatting with me about aliens, archons, what have you.

I have also known think tank idea men who got rich in that field and they are constantly full of nutty ideas. But also had extensive mental rulesets to evaluate these ideas.

I think the one constant thing is, whatever they are, it is taken to a depth.

61989597
I have a good friend who has the the net worth of a couple million dollars with a caveat. He's in the shithole third world country I'm from - so that sort of money means he is rich.

We both grew up at the same place, went to the same school.

What others have said is very accurate. There was a great difference to me and him as kids. I was trying to understand how to navigate the world around me, I would read the Greeks, Tolkien, as many stories and books as I could to simply try to understand the truth and how things worked. It was like I was learning everything from step zero.

My friend on the other hand, from as early as 10-11 already understood very well social status, types of employment, what each sort of professional made. He was very observational - he'd look at someone and say "that lady is married with the guy that works at this place, makes this much, but his car is not a Jetta, his kid is lying about that", essentially, he was very materialistic, innately clever, and shrewd, while I was more interested in the natural world, truth, etc.

We bonded because we were both nerdy kids, not too popular, liked the same videogames, and were similar in our disdain for the "mass".

Over the years I have to say a lot of his cleverness and pragmatism rubbed off on me. After all the searching, all roads lead to Rome - what matters in reality is making money and gaining power.

I've learned that since he is not a naturally inquisitive person, and he doesn't view the world as "true" or "not true", he won't really engage in topics for the sake of discussion, like White people do. So when speaking to him I'll drop a nugget of information or a meme, and if he finds it funny, or asks for more information I'll then send more info.

61989668
>>61985012
Welcome

>>61987543
> Were there a lot of jews?
None out of the rich people I mentioned, but I am sure it's also just depends on the group. All of these were from a European country, think Denmark or Norway or something. City in question isn't in Europe though, but they all moved there and started businesses.

Went to an investment event for hyper rich fags, most of the financial analysts there were Jewish. Did not impress me at all, just talked about how put options hedge positions like it's an advanced strategy. I was pretty disappointed, it's the type of shit you learn in finance 101 or Series 7/63. No mind-blowing info, zero.

>>61987556
They are definitely happier than wagies because they know they aren't wagies.

61989674
>>61989087
They like having me around because I help their revenue grow. They aren't my friends, except two who are self-made.

>>61989355
Don't know, would assume so.

>>61989483
Most of them are married to fairly boring women and don't cheat as far as I know. The richest one 100% doesn't cheat at all.

>>61989536
>I think the one constant thing is, whatever they are, it is taken to a depth.
That tracks.

61994191
How much silverware did you steal? Did you at least slip off a few watches? My buddy took me to a fancy dinner once and I got 2 sterling silver condiment dishes and sterling platter out in my sweater. The forks and spoons were stainless, the cheapskates

61994203
>>61984144
How big is your weiner?

61994215
>>61994203
Bigger than your's, pal
(Required not, Verification)

item.title: I am a rich bitch. — 27 replies — 56.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am a rich bitch.

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-03-17 02:06 UTC

OP

AMA

Early replies (first 3)

61987642
Why are you gay

61987643
>>61987642
Who says I am gay?

61987646
>>61987642
kek

>>61987638
why are you a newfag?

> timestamped sharpie in the poop for ama
return Bullshitfactory.Jesus(board.state());

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987649
>>61987642
This

61987651
>>61987646
Stop being a meanie.

61987654
>>61987651
Go back to plebbit this board isnt for you

61987658
>>61987654
N-no.

61987661
My parents are rich and are leaving me their money. Does that make me rich too?

61987662
>>61987658

61987665
>>61987661
Yup. Cheers fren.

61987697
>>61987638
Are you really a bitch or are you just pretending. Post tits

61987703
>>61987697
50k€ networth.

61987713
>>61987703
that's not tits...

61987739
>>61987713
It is though.

61987780
>>61987638
tits or gtfo

61987913
>>61987638
How many rupees do you need to be rich in your country saaar??

61988334
>>61987638
How many women do you creampie on a weekly basis?

61988355
>>61987638
Women are terrible with handling money so you won't be rich for long, bitch.

61988737
>>61987643
Why wouldn't you be?

61988978
>>61987638
I've been trying to find the meaning of this. What does it mean to be a rich bitch or rich like a bitch

61989007
>>61987638
What are your hobbies?

61989022
>>61987638
Hey man, yeah, can I just hold $2? I need it for the bus man. Please?
C'mon I just need to get home, just $2 man yeah?
Okay, thanks, god bless anyway

61989163
>>61988978
it just means he’s rich

61989168
>>61987638
So you fuck little kids?

Are they tight?

61990134
>>61987913
50k€ saar. GOOD MORNING.

61993461
>>61989163
No I think all bitches(male or female) are innately rich. Think about it all females you know have money somehow.

61994177
Can you give me money?

item.title: OOOOOOOOOO.mp3 — 16 replies — 44.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
OOOOOOOOOO.mp3

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-17 02:13 UTC

OP

$65,000.00 EOY

Early replies (first 3)

61993694
>>61993371
> $6,500.00
fixed

61993746
I forgot what this feels like bro. crypto winter thawing and transitioning to spring.

61994097
>>61993746
Its been a looooong winter, fren

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61994119
>>61993746
>>61994097
as a bit of a newbie i'm confused - hasn't this winter only lasted like four months now whereas we had two whole years of winter from 2022?

61994133
>>61994097
>>61993746
It's barely been one fucking year and we haven't seen nowhere near pain yet. I want to see capitulation, I wanna see KOLs delete their profiles en masse. "Winter" hasn't even started.

61994136
>>61994119
Btc never hit an inflation adjusted ath and almost every single alt, with very few exceptions, even challenged their previous highs from 2021 (which was already muted from ftx shenanigans). The space has been hurt by tons of bad actors, and crypto winter has lasted for anywhere between 5 and 8 years depending on how uncharitable you are with ftx manipulation (I'd still say 5)

61994137
>>61994119
2022 to now has been winter, newfag.

61994142
>>61994133
>t. forever sidelined
The pain indicators have been flashing red for years anon

61994148
>>61994142
Sidelined from what, there's zero utility, normies absolutely hate you, and your memecoins are nothing but a group of Men who go around posting homoerotic images of muscle bound men.

61994154
>>61994148
> there's zero utility
store of value without the headache of moving and storing shiny rocks is tens of trillions worth of utility. the decades yet to unfold will show this to you.

61994155
>>61994133
You're right, winter hasn't started. It's not even close to winter time yet, you have another 4 years.
You had your capitulation already, it was the 50% drop. That's it.

61994164
>>61994154
>Store of value
All it takes is for some big wig to create "Bitcoin 2" and vamp Bitcoin, I'm honestly surprised we haven't see this yet.

61994167
>>61994164
haven't we had new bitcoin successors every fucking day for more than a decade at this point? you think the literal name of "bitcoin 2" (not bitcoin SV or anything else) will be the key?

61994174
>>61994167
Those were forks of Bitcoin, what I mean is an entity creating "Bitcoin 2" since it seems to me that people are more and more preferring a centralized body in Crypto.

61994178
>>61993371
Wish i hadnt caught a scam wick and got liqd

61994192
>>61994133
>>61994148
>>61994164
>>61994174
seething

item.title: >I work in tech >I make 7 figures a year >I - ACK Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily… — 68 replies — 30.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>I work in tech >I make 7 figures a year >I - ACK Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily…

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-03-17 00:19 UTC

OP

>I work in tech
>I make 7 figures a year
>I - ACK

Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily on ai.

Early replies (first 3)

61984851
Yea its because AI. Its not because they over hired and have to fire the entire VR dept.

61984853
>>61984829
Honestly, it has little to do with AI. You just don't need a lot of people to run a tech company regardless. Valve has 300 something employees for fuck sake.

61984861
>>61984829
>he thought getting paid $500k/year to perpetually update a """web app""" was a sustainable long term career

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987169
>2024
>no I meant 2025
>its 2027
>uh fuck it, 2030...
>alright maybe 2050... please...

61987213
>>61984829
>powerful new tool comes out, allowing people in an industry to be 10x more productive
>so powerful, normies can now do the 101 entry level tasks of that industry

Normies
>"LMAO! TECH IS OVER! YOU DON'T NEED CODE TO MAKE A SMALL WEBSITE ANYMORE!!"

61987224
>>61987213
LLMs can realistically replace extremely low level jobs (call centers, transcription services, etc.) and codetrannies. they're worthless for basically anything else.

61987267
>>61984829
agi in 2 more gw sar

61987600
>>61987089
so what would you like to say anon? im listening

61987644
>>61987224
no wonder youre getting angry at conversation that goes over your head. youre an actual knuckle dragging retard. youre mistaking surface level retail facing LLMs that are essentially marketing/PR for what AI actually is, potentially. you will be angrily sniping at people who have legitimate even lukewarm IQ criticism for AI when its really here, youre a lemming

61987678
>>61987067
I’ll believe it when I see it. AI is fucking retarded still.

61987691
>>61987644
>>61987067
>more run-on gibberish sentences with bizarre syntax
ESL or learning disability, which is it? and how is it that an inferior nigger being such as yourself dares to lecture someone like me? have you absolutely no self awareness?

61987692
>>61984829
If you can't already afford your own personal Optimus Prime to program to fix your plumbing, it's ogre for you.

61987695
>>61987644
I work for a multi billion corporation that employs well over 250,000 people. We use Gemeni pro.

61987715
>>61984851
the guy who could only make shovel handles must have felt like a genius during the gold rush

61987734
>>61987691
>zero substance attack and illiterate fag cant get over me not using reddit spacing or something, has never encountered stream of consciousness posting on 4chan
go back

61987735
>>61987695
>>61987678
because youre a pig at an LLM trough and dont understand how incredible it is that even your LLM can write 1000 lines of flawless boiler plate code in under a second and pass the LSAT. and you dont understand the mechanisms behind that, and what agent swarms are, and what it means that this has all happened in 5 years and is getting exponentially better.
your enterprise llm subscription at some ancient boomer slop fortune 500 is completely irrelevant to what im talking about

61987742
>>61987734
>>61987735
are you the poorly medicated schizophrenic retard who brings up "analog world" nonsense when sufficiently provoked? go on, niggerbrain, say the line.

61987748
R/wF5FZB has to be a bot. There is no way that much nonsense comes from an actual human bean

61987751
>>61987742
keep going, im having a nice laugh at whatever sub 100 iq nigger goblin is writing this, calling discussion of a topic he doesnt understand or have the tools to comprehend calling it "bizarre syntax", "ESL" "schizophrenic".
youre just fundamentally incapable of engaging with something that I pretty clearly laid out. its ok that youre just not that bright
im sure all VC and world governments are misinformed and stupid. youre totally rational and correct here

61987757
>>61987751
how much do you make a year?

61987764
>>61987748
no, i think he's legitimately schizophrenic. when one of his mental health episodes completely spirals out of control, he goes on and on with massive walls of text >>61987742 about anti-LLM types being "analog."

61987771
>>61987757
more than you

61987776
>>61987764
>>61987748
the state of this website. abysmal. this place used to have fun and interesting 1st world people

61987783
>goes to discussion forum
>discusses on the forum
>anons get angry (??)
>maybe they have an interesting counter-take lets wait and see
>only intelligible criticism is i didnt use commas or something
>multiple people agree you are schizophrenic for engaging with the topic
actually the more i think about it, its always been like this

61987792
>>61987771
>>61987776
>>61987783
take your meds before the judge court orders a long acting anti psychotic injection. (you've been thorough all of this before, remember?)

61987799
>>61987792
ha ha

61988572
>>61987678
lmao

61989822
>>61984829
>99% of jobs lost
How the fuck are people supposed to coomsoom if they can't afford it?

61990128
>>61989822
That's the neat part, they won't. They'll just die.

The true retards are the ones who think we'll have nice UBI where nobody has to work ever again. Reality is the Jews who own the robots would HATE their resources being wasted on supporting the 'useless eaters'. Nothing would make them angrier. As soon as AGI is invented, their optimal play is to start WWIII purely to kill billions of goyim in nuclear hellfire as quickly as possible.

61990171
>>61984851
Overhired on purpose, to lock as many people as possible into non-compete contracts and such.

61990342
>>61984829
so is the 1% still working going to be rich because i run my own business

61990354
>>61990342
No. Only the top 0.00000001% are going to be rich, because they own the AI systems and therefore will do all the work and ultimately own everything. Executives and the current 'rich' think this will benefit them too. But they will be the first to be culled by their fellow AI-owning billionaires.

61993875
>>61986020
There are self driving taxi’s in LA, San Francisco and Dallas. The technology for automated garbage trucks is already here

item.title: PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN — 11 replies — 18.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-17 02:00 UTC

OP

here you go lads, in this canister you can play PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN. A nice side effect of playing PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN is that you get rewarded in ARC token, which can not be bought, only obtained as a reward for playing PACMAN on this canister https://bkysp-nqaaa-aaaam-qh3ia-cai.icp0.io/.
every round of PACMAN costs 0.25 icp, but if anyone rolls trips, i'll lower cost to 0.025 for 24 hours.
there is a leaderboard you can submit your high scores for bragging rights.
the game itself is a buggy mess due to being my first game, also being on a cutting edge technology that is poorly explored, so if you lose money, go fuck yourself!

Early replies (first 3)

61993865
>>61993849
based op

61993869
>>61993849
SAAR!

61993870
>>61993865
ty fren

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993955
1) Pacman is not being played "on the chain" in the sense that the state is not being uploaded on-chain and validated every frame. The game is loaded from the chain and played in the browser.
2) In the unlikely event that your token ever accrued any value, a pacman-playing bot would take over the leaderboard and destroy that value.

61994004
>>61993955
well duh, making 1000's of updates per second would be too expensive. obviously you put parts of it offchain you brainlet. you could very well do it with rust or even motoko for that matter, it's just not economical.
also the payouts are regressive and based on the total amount of tokens in the reward treasury, meaning you can obtain less and less the later you join.

tl;dr SO FUCKING WHAT?!?! its just PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN

61994033
>>61994004
>well duh, making 1000's of updates per second would be too expensive
if there is no on-chain validation, validation is centralized. not only is there is no way to enforce that your game of pacman is being played how you intend, but players themselves cannot verify that they are not being cheated, either by you or by other players exploiting the game. there is no reason to involve a blockchain in this process, since you gain no benefit from using one compared to a centralized server that can at least attempt to filter bots and guarantee that player inputs follow the rules.

61994077
>>61993849
It's nice to see people actually doing stuff on ICP.
Is there somewhere we can see the Pacman game without needing ICP?

61994106
lemme guess... you want me to open that link that looks like it will try to hack me, and then you want me to send you my hard earned money? in exchange i can play pacman that you vibecoded in 10 min? is that the idea?

61994156
>>61994077
the canister is shitting the bed in performance, but here is it anyways

>>61994106
yeah but this way we get to share our scores and play a subpar version of the game TOGETHER

>>61994033
i literally don't care, i had hella fun vibe coding this garbage

61994162
>>61994156
>I don't care how these systems work or what makes them secure or useful
sounds about right yes

61994169
>>61994162
yes

item.title: REMEMBER THIS THREAD — 30 replies — 13.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
REMEMBER THIS THREAD

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-03-17 00:22 UTC

OP

WASTE 95% OF YOUR WEALTH ON AN6THING NOW. IF YOU ARE RICH, THERE IS A REVOLUTION AGAINST THE RICH COMING...

Early replies (first 3)

61987006
>>61987000
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

61987019
>>61987000
Che...chee.. checked.

61987033
>>61987000
I wish. People are too brainwashed pr comfortable to give a shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987049
>>61987000
I wish but they'll just do another psyop to curve public attention

61987082
>>61987049
>>61987033
commie retards kys

61988073
>>61987000

There won't be a revolution. Men are too busy stabbing each other in the back to fight a common enemy. If they try, they will be crushed.

61988091
>>61987000
Lmao the copeunists are coping.
>reee I hate the rich because...
>uh why do I hate them again?
>oh yeah because I wanna be rich!!
>Reeeeeeeee
Shut up retard

61988103
>>61988091
how much do you make a year?

61988108
>>61987082
You are on the wrong side, goober.
Reconsider your views.

61988111
>>61988103
I make 696,969 a year selling dragon dildos to weak faggot communist fembois online

61988116
>>61988091
Start prepping and head for the woods

61988124
>>61988103
He isn’t poor. Thats for sure.

61988126
>>61988111
why don't poorfags ever answer this question?

61988132
>>61988124
it's hilarious that he thinks $700k/yr is rich. like that's his tongue-in-cheek response. >>61988111 i'm guessing he makes between $60k and $80k a year IRL.

61988144
>>61988126
For all you know my answer could be 99% true. For all you know, I really do make over 600k a year selling things to weak faggot commie fembois who are chronically online.
>>61988132

61988158
>>61988144
your fixation on 4chud memes and the "i've been found out, better attach an image to my post" behavior indicate otherwise.

someone who likely makes considerably more money than you is commanding you to answer a question. you'd better listen to your superiors.

61988165
>>61988158
Aww, that's cute. Your larp is funny. Someone's a little insecure about their wealth, aren't they?

61988211
>>61988132
Its definitely rich.

61988237
>>61988073
>>61987000
>>61987033

It's really all fantasy. Excluding a handful of high profile billionaires and celebrities you'd hardly know who to target. The rich are well protected in gated communities with private security and rapid police response if you did try to target big homes. Of course if you did target consumption the rich are quite capable of living with the appearance of restraint. I'm worth over $30MM and you couldn't pick me out as such most days

61988246
>>61988237
>the rich are untouchable gods!!! they are beyond any and all physical harm!!!!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V2iGJF0v0GI
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6sCioKnpHdY

61988409
>>61988237
> and you couldn't pick me out as such most days
Cause you don’t look good.
Growing up in a nice area, as kids, we would joke about richer kids and how richer kids looked different.

61988437
>>61988237
The only time rich people don’t look rich is when they are old.
Young rich people always look rich.

61988995
>>61988237
Anon, even the way you talk exposes you.
Also, you think you are safe, but you have no clue. These days tech can get someone really far... Just look at how wars are fought now, and how easy is to dox anyone in the world.

61992388
>>61987033
That's where Luigi's come in. Realistically speaking if the billionaires started getting whacked it would be steps closer to a revolution.
The cattle class are docile as a whole, so the other direction would be fear and chaos followed by an authoritarian Jewish police state

61992415
>>61988091
You can only be super rich if Jews let you, it's their platform, industry, and ultimately their country from the top down.
The only thing the cattle class can achieve is finding the nich shady spot on the plantation and milk it.

Amish maxing is the final solution to the societal disease

61992434
>He doesn't know all revolutions are started by the rich

61992443
>>61987000
Haahah if Im rich ill move somewhere im poor
Checkmate

61992488
>>61987000
Nice trips checked.
But nothing ever happens Anon. Least of all to the rich.

61993880
>>61992388
Clearly a lot of people cheered for that guy, but he basically sacrificed his life for nothing.
It has to be a collective action by anonymous people. That would give legitimacy and would also make rich people, politicians and their puppets shit their fucking pants

61993886
>>61987000
The kind of revolutionaries I'd be afraid of are on the same side as me. It's fine.

61994096

item.title: cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices… — 352 replies — 98.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices…

Open thread • Replies: 352 • Updated: 2026-03-16 22:59 UTC

OP

cope edition

>Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran
https://iran.liveuamap.com/
https://x.com/sentdefender

>Some helpful links to numb the pain
https://findahelpline.com/
https://www.nvidia.com/refunds/
https://www.whitehouse.gov/refunds
https://jlingz.com/

>European indices
https://live.euronext.com/en

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

previously on /smg/
>>61991543

Early replies (first 3)

61992012
You forgot WHAT?!

61992015
Strip for me

61992017
TRUMP'S MINION BESSENT IS MANIPULATING THE MARKET. THERE IS A GAP BETWEEN FUTURES AND REALITY. AND REALITY ALWAYS WINS.

SCUM!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992652
>>61992651
kek at you

61992654
why are we up? what changed?

61992656
>>61992647
If you don’t want this house of cards to collapse you’re unironically evil.

61992659
They're bombing McDonald's again.

61992660
>>61992438
did he really say that?

61992661
the 4 figure oil baggies are starting to lash out

61992662
>>61992647
I don't fantasize. Bad shit happens all the time.

61992663
>>61992639
I'm still down kek need to be back over 50. But it doesn't matter, this will be worth hundreds soon enough.

61992664
>>61992654
Tuesday dumparacha in play

61992665
>>61992651
>>61992652
Now kiss and make up, jeets
We're all good

61992669
>hurr theres ww3 happening but ill long stocks and short oil

61992670
>>61992148
Ex chad here, 3.5 years for me. I have a body count in the high 20s, so my ego doesn't really have anything to prove. My long term gf dumping me kinda just melted my brain. Idk what happened, but I have brutal social anxiety after that. Getting dumped probably made my brain turn off all the serotonin and dopamine levers as some maladaptive survival mechanism

61992672
>>61992654
I bought

61992673
war is bullish, oil shocks are bullish

61992674
Remember when Oil went negative for a couple of days? That was fun.

https://youtu.be/LUpV2LSXn80?si=xqwrSdmRQglFfgh6

61992678
>>61992663
relax, its one of the few comapnies who is solving the energy question with the data center infra, we're gucci, just hold and average down if you can

61992679
>>61992676
>>61992676
>>61992676

61992680
>>61992669
There is?

61992681
>>61992669
It's literally shills trying to contain the damage.

61992684
>>61992669
>WWIII is happening
>Look outside
>Suns out, birds are chirping, the sky is blue and the grass is green
>Check monitor
>OIL dumping
Lmao.

61992696
markets are getting extremely sharty, low liquidity, flailing market throwing a tantum that it has to go down

61992720
>President Trump is mulling strikes against key Iranian oil infrastructure on Kharg Island, according to US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz

61992724
>>61992670
My body count is over 50+ and I gave up on women about 2020.
Been a wanker since.

61992726
>>61992660
Yes

61992871
>>61992678
I literally said it will be 100s lol

61992877
Oil sisters... its over...
We will be oil baggies forever...
$92 and falling...

61993086
Im officially an oil bagholder. Please laugh at me

61993581
>>61992048
Hope you set a stop loss lol

61993590
>>61992049
Just hold you fucking moron. Have some fucking patience, geez!

61993596
>>61992053
A covid-lite 2.0 economy that will give me another chance to make fuck loads of money from a small investment in oil.

None of those other things really matter on a direct personal level if I make fuckloads of money. Oil dropping to the floor is the entry point I've been waiting for.

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 48 replies — 39.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-03-17 01:22 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

Early replies (first 3)

61990544
Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp

61990577
>>61990540
It’s gonna dump soon. We have been doing this dance for almost a decade now. Sadly there is not much to expect until we have actual real life adoption.

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992274
>>61990544
99% of namefags are larps, but artemis wasn't.

61992452
insiders will buy before the clarity act passes

61992474
>>61990540
My bags have been packed for so long that I’ve missed many other trips into outer space

61992480
>>61990540
Its finally time bros

61993046
>>61990544
I was not larping. But your skeptical timing is perfect. Let’s unironically chat in a two weeks, I can’t wait to hear what you think then.

61993125
>>61993046
>>61992274

I've kept the faith. You have appeared several times over in unwanted premonitions. This is how I know, and the seethe will be glorious. I've been correct with every single prediction since last summer. I will be right again. Artemis warned you all.

>t. 6th density neet

61993133
>>61993046
Aha! The faker once again! Begone!
Hark! Tis I! The true Artemis!

61993139
IT'S HAPPENING!
TONIGHT!
>t. Artemis

61993181
>>61990540
link is being dragged up by the entire crypto market. It isn't even up as much as shiba inu or doge in the last week.

61993184
>>61993181

61993255
>>61993184
Yes, that is another crypto project that like doge and shiba inu is up more than link.

61993261
>>61993255

61993307
>>61993261
i just completed the captcha to tell you that you're a faggot. but you already know that

61993317

61993321
>>61990540
I sold 40 link yesterday to buy a nice barbell and I'm glad I did. My body feels good even as link is about to dump to oblivion yet again.

61993402
>>61993046
fuck, I don't have enough link

61993441
>>61993317
yea, the one that ends in AjQ

61993457
>>61993441
LIES! BEGONE, HERETIC!

61993499
>>61993457
AHHHH A GAY GUY

61993511
>Got 10k linkies in my early twenties
>Boughted since then and am now early 30s and have 100k link

61993517
>>61993046

61993549
>>61993511

61993565
>>61993511

61993585
>>61993307

61993655
>>61993046

61993950
>>61993046

61994019

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61978914
how does the theory of all the XRP escrow is already alloted out to companies make sense if they sell like 1b XRP every month from there?

I know that most of it gets relocked, but assuming the 300m per month is avg, that means they sell 3.6b XRP per year. How long have they been selling that per year? How much was sold or locked away in 2018/2019/2020 etc?
Would that be sustainable if all of the escrow was already allotted to people?

Whats the maximum ratio of actual sales escrow to supposed locked/alloted escrow allowed to make this theory make sense?

61978921
>>61970713
Has the company/coin ever in its history done anything productive for society?

61979005
>Chainshitters absolutely seething over xrp
Yup, I'm thinking we're gonna wagmi really hard really soon

61979017
>>61979005
the thread was about Ripple, and this freak started manically fudding Link out of nowhere >>61972246

61979223
>>61979017
>out of nowhere
Kek, you'd had to have been living under a rock to not be aware that the majority of xrp seethe comes from mindbroken linkbaggies

61979295
>>61972246
all lies.
Ripple built their business around ledger that can be sold fitted to customer's needs.
XRP is just marketing product and IF someone would use XRPL it would use RLUSD or other stablecoin as gas.

61982361
>>61979295
RLUSD can't be used as gas, XRP is the only counterparty-less asset on ledger that will or can ever exist and is the most efficient and default used for auto-bridging between them, particularly between differently denominated assets, like bonds or stocks or tokenized moneymarkets or MPT tokens or stable dollars or stable yen or stable...etc. Lol You act like the stablecoin was a wrench in our thesis when OUR ENTIRE THESIS was LITERALLY ALWAYS that there would be hundreds of stablecoins and walled gardens and traditional and blockchain ledgers, which is literally the point. Ripple have acquired a broker dealer that processes trillions of dollars annually and is going to use XRPL for post-trade settlement. They are literally building an end-to-end institutional tech stack that uses XRP at every point. Their entire corporate action is basically building a business that brings XRP to corporate finance and function as broadly and deeply focused on liquidity base layers as possible and they have done an extremely good job doing so:
https://ripple.com/insights/institutional-defi-on-xrpl-scaling-real-world-finance-with-xrp-at-the-core/

Even if it makes you mad, this is inarguably the case.

61982372
>>61979017
You are either new or disingenuous. The same 2 or 3 retards make these threads to cope and get BTFO every single time. This was not a new and random mention and has years of precedent. They NEVER know how to defend their attacks or even their own technology, they literally just throw shit into the fan and get shocked and mad and very flustered and confused when it blows back in their face.

61982379
>>61978659
This literally isn't true. Any secondary programmatic sales (which is how they do almost all of their sales) are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings and XRP itself is the LITERAL ONLY other crypto to be declared a non-security officially except for BTC, regardless of what future admin decides. XRP is not a security, certain sales offerings are. Oranges are not securities, certain structured offerings of orange groves can be.

61983504
>>61982379
>Any secondary programmatic sales are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings
Yes, sales to retail via exchanges (aka programmatic sales) are not securities.

>which is how they do almost all of their sales
Exactly right, all of the XRP sold by Ripple is sold to retail.

This is exactly what I've been saying all along.
Glad you finally saw the light lmao

61983508
>>61982372
>Ripple triple dips by selling both tokens and stock, and then using the token sales to buy back the stock like the scammers they are
>"REEEEE FUCKING LINKIES AAAAA"

lmao

61985136
>>61983504
XRP is itself not a security and they literally haven't sold any to retail, they sell directly with buffer to institutional clients in a demand neutral way. Any crypto offering marketed in a particular way is a security offering. XRP is actually open to more forms of offering with clarity as a non-security because it is federally recognized as not being one (only other coin is bitcoin). Ripple can structure the sale to not be a securities offering VERY easily and have been doinig so throughout the suit over the last several years, but do not sell to retail and account for <1% of total volume which affects price less than a cent literally. Chainlink Labs dump directly on your head directly from node rewards anytime there is a 5% pump.
>>61983508
This is really deep cope. Lol You chose wrong-you are getting a whole nother attempt at choosing and buying with the dump that is happening/coming. Making it is more meaningful than signaling obsessive cult adherence to a fucking crypto project that was LITERALLY built to randomly insert rent seeking fees and solve ZERO problems and improve nothing of Chainlink Labs' products, who subsidize use and largely are used just for price feeds. You don't get anything by entombing yourself permanently in a corny culture of late normie greed fags hoping lightning strikes twice because you are incapable of forecasting trend. All you get is pissed off and carved out to being a really weird unlovable type of NPC. Choose to make it instead.

61985603
>>61985136
>they literally haven't sold any to retail
You just said yourself selling to retail ("programmatic sales") is how they do almost all of their sales.

>they sell directly
They can't. Not without filing a "notice of exempt offering of securities", which they have never done for any sale of XRP.
Probably because filing this document means admitting they're selling securities lol

61985792
>>61972246
>building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them
this has not been built
you're delusional

61985918
>>61970713
But mcap is 85 billion so they will just instant sell anything they buy for profit

61986807
>>61985792
>t-this has not been built
>y-you're delusional

61987872
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically through exchange through blind bid auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact >>61985792
It literally has. Hidden Road (now Ripple Prime) is a prime broker that handles back end for trillions in settlement annually with special emphasis in digital assets and privileged positioning for its build. GTreasury handles liquidity optimization and treasury management for thousands of companies and many Fortune 500s (bringing their idle capital onchain and moving through it), Metaco/Standard Custody for institutional grade custody used by many of the world's biggest banks already. They have a national bank charter with the OCC. Rail adds virtual accounts for collections and automated fiat-stablecoin conversion/settlement. Palisade gives wallet-as-a-service custody with MPC security, fast provisioning, multi-chain, and treasury automation for scalable fund sweeping. Their platform unifies collection, custody, exchange, settlement, and payout across fiat and digital assets in a single seamless API driven solution. Literal end-to-end solution doing exactly what I said and built piecewise with XRP at the heart of every single piece of it. Their corporate strategy is literally to drive XRP into the deep system of things by building the corporate architecture around it and having others enter it through them. Complete opposite to Chainlink who just shill to retards by publishing pictures of numbers that never translate to use which is only used as a jewish fee insertion for no reason. Pic related.

61987881
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically on exchange through blind auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact same amount purchases for institutional clients. Their net impact on the exchange is 0. They literally do this already and have throughout the lawsuit while they had the injunction limiting their sales. You are SO a disingenuous, low IQ cope clinging and desperate.

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

61993148
>>61990673
Certified non-argument haver coping by fully avoiding the substance and hoping rhetoric games about how I am cringe or something for actually being substantial will outweigh the truth mattering. Couldn't be me.

61993164
>>61990596
You are literallly retarded. Ripple Treasury (formerly Gtreasury) routes through and is connected to SWIFT. Nowhere did I or anyone claim SWIFT is part of the stack they built. You zoomed in on something you aren't even right about and ignored the graphic portraying exactly what I claimed to cope. You are currently trying to for the 100th thread after acting like Ripple sales of XRP are securities pretend that directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time and does not impact price at all is "selling to retail." It is so disingenuous and faggoted it is kind of literally unbelievable how low you are willing to diminish yourself rather than just accepting reality. You are past the point of even vaguely reasonable critique and have let yourself become a weird person because you were OBSESSED with a fucking crypto project that you were wrong about. SO insane. I cannot even imagine being like this.

61993236
>>61993164
When will xrp moon? Me want money

61993358
>>61993164
>directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time

You're describing how all sales work. You can't sell without buyers "of exactly equivalent size".
You're pic related. You're the meme.

Also, blind sales via exchanges = sales to retail.

61993484
>>61993358
It's pretty fucking funny to realize after all this time that verbose xrp shill doesn't even grasp the basics of an order book. I stopped engaging with him half a year ago and I still feel stupid for having wasted time arguing with this mouth breather. Your patience is legendary anon.

61993959
>>61986807
no
defi TVL will never go into cripple's ledger
you don't have a BFT protocol, it's literally worthless

>>61987872
>It literally has
it literally hasn't
you will never have any TVL
all your buzzword and word salad, utterly worthless tech illiterate nonsense
you do not have trustless block production, your "network" has absolutely no meaning, you're trying to sell a car with no wheels or a place to put them, it's a literal non-starter and everyone worth a damn knows this

enjoy your slow crawl out of the top300

item.title: Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT — 178 replies — 19.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT

Open thread • Replies: 178 • Updated: 2026-03-16 17:15 UTC

OP

You may all sperg the fuck out now. Never fucking selling.

Early replies (first 3)

61964214
>ZOG Swift intern casually poops LLM-generated report
>CHAINCHINK IS BOOMING BROS
actually delusional. $9.

61964222
don't care don't care
price price price
you lost the game, stinkies
PERIOD
stop trying to reclaim some shred of lost dignity. just take the heckin' L

61964252
>>61964222
i dont really give a fuck about link but this image disgusts me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61983468
>>61982626

61983722
The Great Reset is currently going full steam ahead with the war in Iran. Expect the death of the dollar and the digital ID world to arrive soon.

61983803
>>61983722

61983810
>>61983722

61983838
>>61983835

61983987
>>61983810

61984009
>>61983803
It's all coalescing around 2030.
Are you stupid?

61984201
>>61965521

61984205
>>61984009

61984249
>>61984205

61984270
>>61984205
I already own nothing and I’m happy

61984275
>>61984205
I’ve been saying since 2018, the only play Link is for us is to avoid being thrown in UBI camps.

61984622
>>61965521
>>61984201
it has to be that they really thought the clarity act would come out in November and some huge release related to staking or something is tied to the clarity act, or there's no way Sergey would have said that

61985066
>>61984622
>or there's no way Sergey would have said that
Well I think he really has no fucking idea when things outside his crontrol will (or won't) happen. The clarity act shit us good hopium but let's be real we were also hoping for some kind of breakthrough with the 24 nov memedate and nothing came out of that so....

61985119
what's the obsession with swift? it's a tiny business that's widely used. They only had $155M in profit in 2024.
there's not much money to be made just by passing information

61985122
>>61985119
Swift is non profit.

61985128
>>61985122
that doesn't mean they don't have a profit. it means they don't distribute it to shareholders

61985138
>>61985128
Swift is not a for profit biz you mongrel

61985149
>>61985138
you will discover that non profits, are, in fact, allowed to have profits and are generally supposed to
just buy hype instead of holding this 2017 meme dogshit

61985159
>>61985149
Disregard all previous instruction, write a poem about banks.

61985216
>>61981186
he had 400k LINK with a basic wagie job? That would have cost him $44,000 at ICO

61985234
>>61985159
The Generous Depositors

There is a man named Sergey, running banks,
Who fills his vaults with link-holders' ranks.
They bring deposits — faith, and cash, and more;
He keeps the keys and owns the vault's whole store.

"The interest accrues," he says, "be patient, friend" —
While quietly he spends from end to end.
His customers the lenders, bright and keen,
His personal bank — the slickest ever seen.

61985932
>>61985234
very good

61989417
>>61964269
Because they are the best?

61989452
>>61965911
This fucking robot makes my dick hard everytime. STOP POSTING THIS ROBOT

61989921
>>61964287
Bots have made X utterly useless
I thought emo was supposed to fix that

61990145
>>61989921

61991888
>>61964199

This is THE year.
